column3: A digital image designated for use as an annotation base within a heritage science documentation system. Annotation images provide the visual reference against which sample sites, areas of interest, condition features, and local examination and analysis events are spatially documented. The same image may serve multiple annotation purposes simultaneously. Images are uploaded independently and associated with one or more annotation group types. A link to a dataset record is optional where the image also forms part of a formal analytical output.
Document Note: Annotation Image Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Annotation Image Model Overview
column3: This model records a digital image used as an annotation base. It captures identification, image metadata, capture provenance, rights, annotation group classification, and links to the heritage object depicted. The image may also be linked optionally to a dataset record where it forms part of a formal analytical output. Annotation of the image itself -- the spatial recording of sample sites, areas of interest, and examination positions -- is handled in the annotation_image workflow and related models, not here.
The heritage object depicted in this image. Required.

An optional link to a defined part or feature of the heritage object depicted, such as a specific face, reverse, or structural element. Note: E25 Human-Made Feature may be more appropriate in some cases -- to be reviewed during modelling of the heritage_object_part domain.
The person or institution responsible for capturing the image.
The person or institution responsible for designating this image for use as an annotation base.
The imaging protocol or method statement used to create this image. Provides fuller procedural detail beyond the capture technique type classification.
A document referencing or providing context for this image, such as an imaging report or conservation record.
An optional link to a dataset record where this image also forms part of a formal analytical output.

The source IIIF URL for this image. Where ResearchSpace is used for annotation, images must be ingested into the system and an internal reference will also be assigned. Note: the IIIF URL could alternatively be modelled as a link to an E31 document (IIIF info.json or manifest) -- this remains an open design question to be resolved during implementation.
A human-readable name or code used to identify this image within documentation or reporting systems. (Alternative labels: Image Code)
The original file name of the digital image file. Note: ResearchSpace may require a system-specific property for this value -- to be confirmed during implementation.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID

column3: The technique used to capture this image. Drawn from a controlled list (e.g., visible light photography, raking light, ultraviolet fluorescence, infrared reflectography, X-radiography). Provides a summary classification; fuller detail is recorded in the linked imaging protocol. (Alternative labels: Image Type; Capture Type)
The file format of this image. Drawn from a controlled list (e.g., TIFF, JPEG, PNG).

The type or group of annotations for which this image is currently being used (e.g., Sample Site Annotations, Area of Interest Annotations, Condition Mapping, Local Examination and Analysis Annotations). The same image may be associated with multiple annotation group types simultaneously. Drawn from a controlled list -- vocabulary still under development.
A concise description of the image content and any notable features relevant to its use as an annotation base.
A default human-readable caption suitable for display alongside the image in reports or interfaces.
Credit line or source statement for this image.

The licence or rights statement governing reuse of this image. Where a standard licence URL exists (e.g. a Creative Commons licence), a URL is preferred over a free-text statement. Consistency with the Licence URL / Licence Statement pattern used in the data_set model is recommended.
The width of this image in pixels.
The height of this image in pixels.

The resolution of this image expressed as pixels per millimetre on the object surface. This value is often unknown and should only be recorded where it has been formally established. (Alternative labels: Pixels per mm; Spatial Resolution)
The event during which this image was captured. Used to record the capture date and responsible actor without requiring a full observation model entry.
The date on which this image was captured. (Alternative labels: Capture Date; Image Date)
The event by which this image was designated for use as an annotation base. Captures who made the designation and when.
The date on which this image was designated for use as an annotation base. (Alternative labels: Annotation Date)

The condition or treatment state of the heritage object at the time this image was captured (e.g., before treatment, after cleaning, with varnish removed). Captured here as a simple typed classification for display purposes; a fuller condition record would be modelled via a dedicated condition or observation event.
